meta_title: Impact Doors in Plantation, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Plantation, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, permits handled. Free quotes — financing from $87/mo.
h1: Plantation's Trusted Source for Hurricane Impact Doors That Protect What Matters Most
intro:
Plantation, Florida sits in the heart of Broward County — a city of wide, tree-lined streets, thriving neighborhoods like Jacaranda, Central Park, and Pine Island Ridge, and homes that represent some of the most significant real estate investments in the region. With an average home value of $430,000 and a community that takes real pride in its properties, Plantation homeowners understand the difference between a home that's simply house-shaped and one that's truly built to last. A critical part of that distinction — one that too many homeowners overlook until it's too late — is the quality and resilience of your entry, sliding glass, and French doors. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we bring certified hurricane-rated impact doors to Plantation residents who refuse to leave their most valuable asset unprotected.

Located approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Plantation may not be a beachfront city, but make no mistake — when a major hurricane moves through South Florida, the inland communities of Broward County are far from safe. The city falls within a 170 mph wind design zone, and Florida Building Code mandates that any new or replacement exterior door installation in this area meet hurricane-resistant product specifications. That means standard builder-grade doors, however attractive they may look, simply do not qualify. What's required are impact-rated doors engineered to withstand the tremendous pressure, debris impact, and sustained wind loads that come with a direct or near-direct hurricane strike — and that's exactly what Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers.

Nearly 45% of homes in Plantation were built before 1994, the year that hurricane-resistant construction standards were dramatically overhauled following the catastrophic lessons of Hurricane Andrew. Many of those older homes in Plantation Isles, Plantation Acres, and neighborhoods surrounding Deicke Auditorium and Volunteer Park were constructed with hollow-core or basic solid wood entry doors, single-pane sliding glass doors, and French door systems that offer virtually no meaningful hurricane protection. Upgrading to modern impact doors is one of the most intelligent, high-return investments a Plantation homeowner can make — providing storm security, energy efficiency, noise reduction, and meaningful homeowner's insurance savings all at once.

section_why.h2: Why Plantation Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties on a single foundational promise: no subcontractors, no shortcuts, and no compromises. Every impact door we install in Plantation is handled entirely by our in-house team of certified installation technicians — professionals who collectively bring more than 60 years of combined experience to every project. That distinction matters more than most homeowners realize. When a company outsources its labor, accountability becomes fragmented, quality control suffers, and the homeowner is often left holding the bag when problems arise. With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the same team that sold you the project installs it, inspects it, and stands behind it.

We've completed over 500 homes across South Florida, and our work in Plantation reflects a deep familiarity with the city's architectural variety — from the ranch-style homes of Central Park to the larger estate properties of Pine Island Ridge and the classic Florida designs throughout Jacaranda. We understand that impact door installation in Plantation isn't one-size-fits-all. Entry door sizes, frame conditions, threshold configurations, and architectural aesthetics vary significantly from block to block. Our team conducts thorough pre-installation assessments to ensure every door we install fits perfectly, seals completely, and operates smoothly for the life of the product.

As an A+ BBB-rated company, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds itself to a standard of transparency that begins at the very first consultation. We walk Plantation homeowners through their product options, explain the permit process through the City of Plantation Building Division, and provide itemized proposals — no vague estimates, no surprise line items. Our team handles all permit submissions and inspections on your behalf, so you're never navigating the bureaucratic side of compliance alone. We believe that earning the title of 'The Kings of Service' means owning every step of the process, start to finish.

From the moment you request your free quote through the final walkthrough inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages your impact door project with the professionalism and attention to detail that Plantation's upper-middle-class homeowners rightfully expect. We don't just install doors — we upgrade homes, protect families, and build the kind of long-term relationships that keep our clients referring their neighbors, friends, and family members throughout Broward County.

section_history.h2: Plantation's Hurricane History Makes a Compelling Case for Impact Door Upgrades
section_history.body:
Plantation has been tested by serious storms no fewer than nine times since 1990, and each event has left its mark on how the community thinks about home protection. The most formative of these was Hurricane Andrew in August of 1992 — a Category 5 storm that, while delivering its most catastrophic damage to Miami-Dade County to the south, sent destructive wind bands and debris fields through Broward County communities including Plantation. Andrew was the event that permanently changed Florida's residential building code, exposing the catastrophic inadequacy of the construction standards that had governed South Florida homebuilding for decades. For the roughly 45% of Plantation homes built before that storm, Andrew remains a sobering reminder of what inadequate door and window protection can cost.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 struck Broward County with particular ferocity — one of the fastest-intensifying and fastest-moving hurricanes in recorded Atlantic history. Wilma made landfall on the southwest Florida coast but tore across the peninsula at highway speed, delivering sustained winds of over 100 mph to Broward County communities and generating massive pressure differentials that blew out sliding glass doors, compromised French door seals, and sent debris crashing through conventional entry doors across neighborhoods like Plantation Acres and Plantation Isles. The storm caused billions in insured losses across the region.

Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017 — a storm that tracked directly up Florida's spine, affecting virtually every community in Broward County. Plantation residents experienced widespread power outages, fallen trees, and structural damage. Homeowners who had already upgraded to impact doors reported dramatically better outcomes than neighbors with older door systems. These storms are not historical anomalies — they are the recurring reality of living in South Florida, and they represent exactly why impact door upgrades in Plantation are not optional luxuries but practical necessities.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Plantation and Broward County
section_code.body:
Plantation sits within Broward County's wind-borne debris region and is subject to Florida Building Code requirements that mandate hurricane-resistant opening protection for all new construction and qualifying replacements. The city carries a design wind speed of 170 mph, which means every exterior door — entry, sliding glass, or French — must be rated to perform at that wind load threshold. While Plantation falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that governs Miami-Dade and the coastal portions of Broward County, the products installed here must still carry Florida Product Approval and meet the performance standards outlined in the Florida Building Code, Residential Volume, Chapter 16.

All impact door replacements in Plantation require a permit issued through the City of Plantation Building Division. This includes permit application, product approval documentation, and a final inspection upon completion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of this process on behalf of our clients — from submitting the permit application with the required product approval numbers and installation drawings, to scheduling and passing the final field inspection. Many homeowners are surprised to learn that unpermitted impact door installations are not only a code violation but can void product warranties and create serious complications at the time of home sale or insurance claim.

The products Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs in Plantation are tested and certified to meet or exceed the standards required for the 170 mph wind zone. Our ESW and CWI door systems carry current Florida Product Approval documentation, ensuring full compliance with both state code and local Broward County amendments. We never install products that cut corners on ratings, and our permitting track record with the City of Plantation Building Division reflects an installation process that consistently passes inspection on the first visit — a standard of workmanship that not every impact contractor in the area can honestly claim.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Product Lines Built for Plantation's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two industry-leading manufacturers — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which offer product lines specifically engineered for Florida's most demanding wind and impact requirements. These are not off-the-shelf big-box products. They are purpose-built hurricane-rated door systems designed to meet the rigorous performance standards that South Florida's storm environment demands, and they carry the Florida Product Approval certifications required for permitted installation in Plantation and throughout Broward County.

Our ESW and CWI impact entry doors feature heavy-gauge aluminum or reinforced fiberglass frames with multi-point locking systems that secure the door at multiple points along the frame — not just at the handle — creating a unified seal that resists both wind pressure and forced entry attempts. The laminated impact glass used in these doors consists of two panes of tempered glass bonded with a shatter-resistant interlayer that holds the glass together even under extreme impact force. This construction is what allows impact doors to pass the Miami-Dade large-missile impact test, one of the most stringent product qualification standards in the country. For Plantation homeowners with expansive rear lanais and pool decks, our CWI and ESW sliding glass door systems offer wide-panel configurations that preserve the open, airy aesthetic of Florida living without sacrificing storm protection.

French door options from our ESW and CWI lines are also popular in Plantation's larger homes, particularly in Pine Island Ridge and Jacaranda, where architectural details matter as much as performance. These systems feature matching multi-point locking hardware, flush threshold options for ADA compliance, and a range of interior and exterior finish options. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door or upgrading every exterior opening on your property, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will match you with the right ESW or CWI product configuration for your home's specific requirements.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Plantation — and Why the Investment Pays for Itself
section_cost.body:
In a city where the average home value sits at $430,000, protecting your property with certified impact doors isn't just a safety decision — it's a sound financial strategy. The cost of impact door installation in Plantation varies based on the number of openings, door type and size, frame condition, and the product configuration selected. Entry door replacements typically range from $1,500 to $4,000 per opening, while sliding glass door systems — particularly wide multi-panel configurations common in Plantation's pool homes — may range from $3,000 to $8,000 or more depending on size. French door systems fall in a similar range. A full home door upgrade for a mid-size Plantation residence often totals between $8,000 and $20,000, a figure that represents a fraction of the home's market value and a small premium compared to the cost of storm damage, emergency repairs, or a denied insurance claim.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these upgrades financially accessible through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down available to qualified homeowners. We work with multiple lending partners to offer terms that fit a range of budgets, and our team will walk you through financing options during your free consultation — no pressure, no obligation, just clarity. Many of our Plantation clients are pleasantly surprised to find that monthly financing payments for a complete impact door upgrade are comparable to or less than the insurance savings they begin receiving immediately upon installation.

The return on investment for impact doors in Plantation is compelling on multiple fronts. In addition to wind mitigation insurance discounts — which we detail in the following section — impact doors reduce energy transfer, lowering cooling costs in South Florida's relentless summer heat. They also eliminate the need for hurricane shutters, saving you time, physical labor, and the ongoing cost of shutter maintenance. Real estate data consistently shows that impact-protected homes command higher resale prices and sell more quickly in competitive South Florida markets, making your door upgrade a value-add that benefits you whether you stay in your home or eventually sell.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums in Plantation
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially tangible benefits of upgrading to impact doors in Plantation is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums that typically follows. Florida's insurance market has undergone significant turbulence in recent years, with carriers raising rates, reducing coverage options, or exiting the state market entirely. For Plantation homeowners, this means that anything that reduces your property's risk profile — including the installation of certified impact-rated openings — carries real monetary value. Florida Statute 627.0629 requires insurers to offer discounts, credits, or other rate adjustments for homes with verified hurricane-resistant construction features, including impact-rated doors and windows.

The specific discount a Plantation homeowner receives depends on their individual carrier and policy structure, but wind mitigation credits for fully impact-protected homes can reduce the wind portion of a premium by 25% to 45% or more. To access these discounts, homeowners typically need a wind mitigation inspection conducted by a licensed inspector who verifies that all exterior openings meet the required opening protection standards. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides product documentation and installation records that make this inspection process straightforward. Many of our Plantation clients recover a significant portion of their investment through reduced insurance premiums over a five to seven year period.

Common insurers operating in the Plantation and Broward County market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation credits under Florida's standardized wind mitigation form (OIR-B1-1802). We strongly encourage every Plantation homeowner who completes an impact door installation with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing to promptly schedule a wind mitigation inspection and submit the results to their carrier. The savings are real, they are recurring, and they begin the very next renewal cycle after your inspection is on file.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Plantation if I'm not near the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Plantation sits approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coast and falls within Broward County's wind-borne debris region with a 170 mph design wind speed. Hurricanes Andrew, Wilma, and Irma all delivered significant wind and debris damage to inland Plantation neighborhoods. Distance from the coast does not meaningfully reduce your exposure to hurricane-force winds or the debris they carry. Florida Building Code requires hurricane-rated opening protection for all exterior doors in Plantation, regardless of coastal proximity.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost for a Plantation home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Cost varies by door type, size, and quantity, but Plantation homeowners typically invest between $1,500 and $4,000 per entry door opening and $3,000 to $8,000 or more for sliding glass door systems. A full home upgrade often ranges from $8,000 to $20,000. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants. We'll review all financing options during your free, no-obligation consultation and help you find a payment plan that works for your budget.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my exterior doors in Plantation, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes. All exterior door replacements in Plantation require a permit issued by the City of Plantation Building Division, along with Florida Product Approval documentation and a final field inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process for you — we submit the permit application, provide all required documentation, and schedule and pass the final inspection on your behalf. Unpermitted installations can void warranties and create complications during home sales or insurance claims, so proper permitting is non-negotiable for us.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Plantation?
faqs.a: Most Plantation impact door installations are completed within one to two days once permits are approved. The permitting timeline through the City of Plantation Building Division typically runs two to four weeks depending on current workload. Our team will give you a realistic project timeline at your consultation and keep you updated throughout the permit approval process. Because we use only in-house installation crews, we have direct control over scheduling and can usually begin installation within days of permit issuance.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Plantation?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — often significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits for homes with certified impact-rated opening protection. Plantation homeowners with fully impact-protected homes — doors and windows — can see the wind portion of their premium reduced by 25% to 45% or more. After installation, you'll need a licensed wind mitigation inspector to document your upgrades on the standard OIR-B1-1802 form and submit it to your insurer. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all product documentation needed to support that inspection.
